scorecards.title: Tree Planting & Urban Canopy
scorecards.description: Tracking Cerritos's urban forestry program including new tree plantings, canopy coverage, and tree health maintenance across the city's parks and streets.
scorecards.tldr: Cerritos is planting more trees each year and slowly growing its urban canopy to cool streets and clean air.
highlights: 450 new trees planted — up 12% from last year
$2.5M invested in tree care, highest budget in 5 years
Shade coverage along South Street improved by 15%
challenges: Still 7 percentage points below the 25% canopy target
Drought-stress causing more tree removals than planned
Need more drought-tolerant species in new plantings

source.documentName: City of Cerritos FY 2024-25 Adopted Budget

scorecards.title: Recycling & Waste Diversion
scorecards.description: Evaluating Cerritos's recycling effectiveness, contamination rates, and progress toward state-mandated waste diversion goals.
scorecards.tldr: Recycling rates are improving but contamination is still an expensive problem costing the city extra money.
highlights: Waste diversion up to 62% — best year yet for Cerritos
Composting participation jumped 8% after new green bin program
Processing costs dropping as contamination decreases
challenges: 22% contamination rate is still double the target
13 percentage points below the 75% diversion goal
Need better outreach to apartment complexes

source.documentName: City of Cerritos FY 2024-25 Adopted Budget

scorecards.title: Water Conservation & Efficiency
scorecards.description: Monitoring Cerritos's water usage trends, conservation program participation, and progress toward state water reduction mandates.
scorecards.tldr: Water use is dropping thanks to rebate programs and drought landscaping, but there's still a long way to go.
highlights: 320 water-saving rebates issued — toilets, washers, and irrigation
challenges: Still 25 gallons above the 100 gpcd state target
Rebate program not reaching 100% of budget allocation
Aging pipes causing more system leaks
